title: Minimum Rounds to Complete All Tasks
description:
You are given a 0-indexed integer array tasks, where tasks[i] represents the difficulty level of a task. In each round, you can complete either 2 or 3 tasks of the same difficulty level.

Return _the minimum rounds required to complete all the tasks, or_ -1 _if it is not possible to complete all the tasks._

solutionCode.python:
```python
def numOfBeams(bank):
    m = len(bank)
    n = len(bank[0])
    beams = 0

    for i in range(m):
        for j in range(n):
            if bank[i][j] == '1':
                k = j + 1
                while k < n and bank[i][k] != '1':
                    beams += bank[i][k] == '0'
                    k += 1
                k = i + 1
                while k < m and bank[k][j] != '1':
                    beams += bank[k][j] == '0'
                    k += 1

    return beams
```
    
    We are given a bank floor plan where '0' represents an empty cell and '1' represents a cell with a security device. We have to find the number of laser beams between any two security devices. The laser beams are independent and do not interfere or join with one another.

The algorithm iterates through each cell in the 2D matrix. If the cell has a security device (i.e., its value is '1'), we check the cells to the right and below the current cell until we find another security device. For each empty cell (i.e., cells with '0') between security devices, we increment the beam count. Once we reach another security device, we move on to the next cell in the floor plan. 

After iterating through all cells, we return the total number of laser beams in the bank's floor plan.
